questions.prompt: Emma visits Yasmine's school. She asks the headmaster: "___ I see the computer room, please?"
questions.explanation: To ask for permission we use "Can I…?": Can I see the computer room? "Want" is not a question word, "behind" is a preposition of place, and "slowly" is an adverb.
After "want" plus a verb we use "to" + the base form: she wants to see the library. "Want see" drops the "to", and "want seeing" or "want to seeing" wrongly add -ing.

questions.prompt: Look at the picture: the playground is at the back of the school. Complete: "The playground is ___ the school building."
questions.explanation: "At the back of" means "behind": the playground is behind the building. "In front of" is the opposite (facing it), "under" means below, and "between" needs two things on each side.

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ maps are there on the classroom wall?"
questions.explanation: "Maps" is a countable noun (one map, two maps), so we ask with "How many": How many maps are there? "How much" is only for uncountable nouns like water or money.

questions.prompt: The suitcase is very heavy. Complete: "Karim ___ carry it; it is too heavy for him."
questions.explanation: The suitcase is too heavy, so Karim is NOT able to carry it: he cannot carry it. "Can" means he is able to (the opposite here), and "wants"/"likes" talk about desire, not ability.
